Baseline Characteristics
2006
2024
Statistical Disclosure Control Active
Secondary disclosure control: ICBs with fewer than 10 events or fewer than 100 procedures in the selected period are automatically suppressed and hidden from the map and table to prevent identification of small numbers. The year range must span at least 2 years.
ICB Rankings — Knee PE (Crude Rate)
Age-Sex Standardised Post-Discharge 90-Day PE Rate (%)
Age-Sex Standardised Post-Discharge 90-Day Bleeding Rate (%)
Annual Procedure Volume
Self-controlled crossover design: Each 7-day post-operative block is paired with a matched 7-day control block 180 days later. Odds ratios >1 indicate elevated post-operative risk relative to the patient's own baseline. COVID period (2020–2021) excluded.
Weekly Post-Discharge OR — PE
Weekly Post-Discharge OR — Bleeding
6-Week Block Summary (Table 2)
Bleeding subtype definitions follow Hammami et al. (Heart, 2025). Non-traumatic bleeding is classified into Upper GI, Lower GI, Haemorrhagic stroke, Haematuria, Respiratory, and Other. Note that subtypes are not mutually exclusive — a single event may be coded under multiple subtypes.
Bleeding Subtypes — Knee Replacement
Bleeding Subtypes — Hip Replacement
Subtype Counts and Proportions
Sensitivity analyses assess the robustness of findings. The COVID sensitivity compares period odds ratios with and without 2020–2021 data. Reference period: 2006–2009.
COVID Sensitivity — Period Odds Ratios (vs 2006–2009)
